channel.title: Laravel on mgalalm - blog

item.title: Laravel Testing Mastering Exception Scenarios
item.description:
Laravel Testing: Mastering Exception Scenarios

In Laravel development, testing is paramount for ensuring your application behaves as expected, especially when dealing with exceptions. Whether they’re custom or built-in, exceptions are an integral part of robust error handling. Let’s explore how to effectively test for both custom and non-custom exceptions in Laravel.

Understanding Exceptions in Laravel

Exceptions in Laravel fall into two categories:

  1. Non-Custom Exceptions: These are PHP’s built-in exceptions like Exception, InvalidArgumentException, etc., which Laravel extends or uses.
